t_ch: 原来只是恐高吗…
t_en: So it's just a fear of heights?

t_ch: 演出了身受重伤的样子。
t_en: You sure acted like you were seriously injured.

t_ch: 呵呵，你们不必再要强了，真正的强者已经到场。我们会像捏住铁饼一样把怪物捏个稀碎，对不对，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: Hehe, you don't have to keep pretending to be strong. The real champion is here. We'll crush the monsters like we're holding a discus, right, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 是的，即将到来的是战斗环节。好在你身边的伙伴个个身怀绝技，你或许可以求助于他们？
t_en: Yes, the battle phase is about to begin. Fortunately, the surrounding companions are all skilled in their own ways, you might want to ask for their help?

t_ch: 自己动手。
t_en: Handle it yourself.

t_ch: 没错，热血贲张、拳拳到肉的战斗才是英雄的浪漫，你的球棒渴望着一场酣畅淋漓的战斗。
t_en: That's right, the passionate, intense battles with every punch landing are the true romance of a hero. Your bat is craving for a thrilling, full-on fight.

t_ch: 问问克利克。
t_en: Ask Kilik.

t_ch: 这您就问对人了，我别的没有，就是鬼主意多。
t_en: You've come to the right person for this, I don't have much, but I sure have a lot of clever ideas.

t_ch: 现在我居高临下，如果您能把怪物们引到这里，我就可以用货箱把它们砸晕。
t_en: Now that I'm in a high position, if you can lure the monsters here, I can use the cargo box to knock them out.

t_ch: 不过这要考验您的身法。假如不慎被追上，恐怕就免不了一场苦斗了。
t_en: But this will test your agility. If you're not careful and get caught, it's bound to turn into a tough fight.

t_ch: 我的操作是一流的。
t_en: My skills are top-notch.

t_ch: 您自由发挥就是，我会做好准备，依照您的步调行动！
t_en: You take the lead, and I'll be ready to follow, acting in sync with your moves!

t_ch: 还是想想更可靠的办法吧。
t_en: Maybe we should think of a more reliable plan.

t_ch: 交给柯库翁。
t_en: Leave it to Glykon.

t_ch: 呃，真、真让我来？
t_en: Uh, really, me?

t_ch: 让怪物见识见识人类的极限！
t_en: Let's show the monsters the power of humanity!

t_ch: 再考虑考虑。
t_en: Let me think it over again.

t_ch: 为了捍卫竞技场英雄的荣耀，短暂的沉思后，柯库翁冲了出去。
t_en: To defend the honor of the arena hero, after a brief moment of contemplation, Glykon charged out.

t_ch: 神殿中的怪物都被扫荡干净，克利克搀扶着刻牢刻走下了楼梯。
t_en: The monsters in the temple were all wiped out, and Kilik helped Kelok down the stairs.

t_ch: 克利克瞅准机会丢下货箱，被砸中的怪物们应声倒地。你和柯库翁一拳一个，将圣殿中的怪物扫荡了个干净。
t_en: Kilik seized the opportunity and dropped the cargo box, crushing the monsters it hit, causing them to collapse. You and Glykon each took down a monster with a punch, clearing out the remaining creatures in the temple.

t_ch: 克利克搀扶着刻牢刻走下了楼梯。
t_en: Kilik helped Kelok down the stairs.

t_ch: 「呃啊！喝——呃啊！看打！」远处传来缠斗声。
t_en: "Ugh! Hah—ugh! Take that!" The sounds of a struggle echoed from the distance.

t_ch: 不忍卒看的残酷战斗结束了，柯库翁一瘸一拐地回到你身边。
t_en: The brutal battle, too hard to watch, finally ended, and Glykon hobbled back to your side, limping.

t_ch: 哈哈，有点棘手，不过不是我的对手。
t_en: Haha, it was a bit tricky, but they were no match for me.

t_ch: 神殿中的怪物都被扫荡干净，克利克搀扶着刻牢刻走下了楼梯。
t_en: The monsters in the temple were all wiped out, and Kilik helped Kelok down the stairs.

t_ch: 呼，多谢两位，能踩在地面上，感觉好多了。
t_en: Phew, thanks to both of you. Being back on solid ground feels so much better.

t_ch: 你们怎么在这儿？
t_en: What are you guys doing here?

t_ch: 哈哈，自从我们不做卖盾贩矛的生意之后，就在构思一些新的小玩意儿。
t_en: Haha, ever since we stopped the spear-and-shield business, we've been coming up with some new little gadgets.

t_ch: 这次我们想到的好点子是——黎明机器灯罩！让您的蜡烛如黎明机器般散发光辉~所以，就来圣殿谈合作了。
t_en: The great idea we've come up with this time is Dawn Device! Let your candles shine with the brilliance of a dawn machine. So, we came to the temple to discuss a partnership.

t_ch: 不过，还没轮到我们觐见祭司，天灾就降临了。大家慌不择路，大多跑进圣殿的后厅，我们跑上了二楼…那种事，实在不想再经历一次了。
t_en: However, before it was our turn to meet with the priest, the disaster struck. Everyone panicked and fled, most of them running into the back hall of the temple, while we made our way to the second floor... I really don't want to go through that kind of thing again.

t_ch: 后厅里还有着其他避难者？至少还有两波黑潮袭击，如果它们冲进后厅，可就糟糕了。
t_en: Are there other refugees in the back hall? There are still at least two waves of the black tide coming. If they break into the back hall, things could get really bad.

t_ch: 我们必须把这里守住。
t_en: We must hold this place.

t_ch: 我们也来帮忙！世界都要重启了，最后的时刻不疯狂一把，未免也太亏了。大不了来世再见嘛！
t_en: We'll help too! The world is about to reset, and if we don't go all out in these final moments, it'd be such a waste. Hey, worst case scenario, we'll meet again in the next life!

t_ch: 你们先去对面避难吧。
t_en: You guys should go take shelter across the way first.

t_ch: 呵呵，我们先去避难？临阵脱逃可不是我们的作风！哪怕是卖假货被抓，我们也是坦坦荡荡，从不退缩。
t_en: Hehe, go take shelter first? Fleeing in the face of danger is not our style! Even if we were caught selling counterfeit goods, we'd face it head-on without hesitation.

t_ch: 再说了，世界都要重启了，最后的时刻不疯狂一把，未免也太亏了。大不了来世再见嘛！
t_en: Besides, the world is about to reset. If we don't go all out in these final moments, it'd be such a waste. Hey, worst case scenario, we'll meet again in the next life!

t_ch: 算了，再创世之后再相见吧。
t_en: Never mind, we'll meet again after the Era Nova.

t_ch: 喂，反正世界都要重启了，最后的时刻不疯狂一把，未免也太亏了。大不了来世再见嘛！
t_en: Hey, anyway, the world is about to reset, and if we don't go all out in these final moments, it'd be such a waste! Hey, worst case scenario, we'll meet again in the next life!

t_ch: 而且，必须要报仇雪恨…可恶的怪物，害得我们的翻本计划全泡汤了！
t_en: And we must avenge ourselves... those stupid monsters ruined our whole plan to make a comeback!

t_ch: 我也要报仇…可恶的怪物，我想好说服祭司的说辞全没用了！
t_en: I want to get my revenge too... those blasted monsters ruined all the reasoning I had prepared to convince the priest!

t_ch: 我也要报仇…可恶的怪物，我还没赢得掷铁饼项目的第四枚个人金牌！
t_en: I want to get my revenge too... those bloody monsters, I still haven't won my fourth individual gold medal in the discus event!

t_ch: 复仇者们，集结！
t_en: Avengers, assemble!

t_ch: 诸位有什么妙计？
t_en: Do any of you have a brilliant plan?

t_ch: 人手和物资都不充裕，即便有如我这样的强者坐镇，恐怕也很难挡住黑潮啊。
t_en: We're short on both manpower and supplies. Even with someone as strong as me in charge, it's going to be tough to hold off the black tide.

t_ch: 我们之前被元老院没收的假货…呃，货物，就放在附近，我们可以把它取来。
t_en: The counterfeit goods... uh, the merchandise that was confiscated by the Council of Elders before, are nearby. We can go retrieve them.

t_ch: 其中有一些武器，可以武装灾民，还有一些防具，可以堆成街垒……
t_en: There are some weapons among them that we can use to arm the refugees, and some armor that can be stacked to make barricades...

t_ch: 呵呵，没错，就是以吉奥里亚指甲制成，什么都能抵挡住的「最强之盾」——
t_en: Hehe, that's right, the "strongest shield" made from Georios' nails, capable of withstanding anything—

t_ch: 以及以尼卡多利长矛铸造，什么都能击破的「最强之矛」！
t_en: And the "strongest spear," forged from Nikador's pikes, capable of piercing through anything!

t_ch: 我就知道。
t_en: I knew it.

t_ch: 听着就很像假货啊！
t_en: Sounds like counterfeit goods to me!

t_ch: 你们库存还挺多的。
t_en: You sure have a lot of stock left.

t_ch: 嗨，现在可不是挑三拣四的时候！
t_en: Hey, now's not the time to be picky!

t_ch: 库房不远，在我们回来前，请务必守住这里！
t_en: The storage room isn't far. Please hold the line until we get back!

t_ch: 刻牢刻和克利克钻进草丛不见了，远处传来令人悚然的呼号，随后，泛着刺目红光的黑色乱流汹涌而来。
t_en: Kelok and Kilik disappeared into the bushes, vanishing from sight. In the distance, a chilling howl echoed, followed by a surge of black turbulence, glowing with an ominous red light, rushing forward.

t_ch: 又一次袭击来临了。
t_en: Another wave of the attack has begun.

t_ch: 呵呵，讨打！我们会像掷链球一样把怪物抛进水沟，对不对，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: Hehe, time for a beating! We'll toss these monsters into the ditch like we're throwing a hammer, right, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 是的，又是战斗环节。好在你身边有一位超越了人类极限的可靠伙伴，或许你可以向他请求帮助。
t_en: Yes, it's another battle phase. Fortunately, you have a reliable companion by your side, someone who has surpassed human limits. Perhaps you can ask for their help.

t_ch: 他原本就是为此而来的，不是吗？
t_en: He came for this exact reason, didn't he?

t_ch: 自己动手。
t_en: Handle it yourself.

t_ch: 你突然想起来自己好像还缺些养成材料。你知道的，养成材料永远都不会嫌太多。
t_en: You suddenly remember that you still need some upgrade materials. You know how it is. There's no such thing as too many upgrade materials.

t_ch: 问问克利克。
t_en: Ask Kilik.

t_ch: 你可以询问克利克，可克利克已经不在你身边了。
t_en: You could ask Kilik, but Kilik is no longer by your side.

t_ch: 不能每次都指望他人的相助，明白这一点，就已经踏出了成长的第一步。
t_en: You can't always rely on others for help. Understanding this is already the first step toward growth.

t_ch: 交给柯库翁。
t_en: Leave it to Glykon.
Me!?

t_ch: 上吧！以健美形态出击！
t_en: Go for it! Charge in with your bodybuilding form!

t_ch: 再考虑考虑。
t_en: Let me think it over again.

t_ch: 为了捍卫竞技场英雄的荣耀，短暂的沉思后，柯库翁冲了出去。
t_en: To defend the honor of the arena hero, after a brief moment of contemplation, Glykon charged out.

t_ch: 你如砍瓜切菜般将怪物收拾干净。
t_en: You cut through the monsters like slicing through melons and vegetables, effortlessly clearing them out.

t_ch: 嚯，好身手。如果你去参加摔跤比赛，恐怕连我也要暂避锋芒。
t_en: Impressive moves there! If you ever entered a wrestling competition, I'd have to step out of your way.

t_ch: 等你先休息一阵子，我们再制定下一步计划吧。
t_en: Why don't you rest a bit? We can work out our next steps afterward.

t_ch: 「呃啊！噢唔——呃啊！别打这里！」远处传来缠斗声。
t_en: "Ugh! Oww — Argh! Not that spot!" The sounds of combat drifted from the distance.

t_ch: 不忍卒看的残酷战斗结束了，柯库翁一瘸一拐地回到你身边。
t_en: The brutal battle, too hard to watch, finally ended, and Glykon hobbled back to your side, limping.

t_ch: 哈哈，有点棘手，不过，咳——不是我的对手。
t_en: Haha, that was a bit tough, but uh... *coughs* they were no match for me.

t_ch: 对你刮目相看了！
t_en: I must say, I'm truly impressed!

t_ch: 我真心想要你的签名了。
t_en: I'd love to get your autograph now, seriously.

t_ch: 包的，{F#姐们}{M#兄弟}。包的。
t_en: Got you covered, {F#miss}{M#dude}. All yours.

t_ch: 咳——我、我先休息一阵子，待会儿再制定下一步计划……
t_en: *coughs* I need to... catch my breath for a bit. We can plan our next move afterward...

t_ch: 等他们回来的这段时间里，我们也不能闲着。
t_en: We can't just sit around doing nothing while we wait for them to come back.

t_ch: 为他们祈祷吧…
t_en: Let's pray for them...

t_ch: 挑战人的极限，捍卫人的尊严，「奥凯纳斯精神」绝不允许我们向神明摇尾乞怜。
t_en: Pushing human limits and upholding human dignity — the Okeanas spirit forbids us from begging the gods for mercy.

t_ch: 出去巡征追猎！
t_en: Let's pursue and hunt them down!

t_ch: 呃…还是不要采取这么激进的战术吧？
t_en: Um... should we really take such an aggressive approach?

t_ch: 附近原本便存放着一些盾牌，在那两个小贩回来前，我们可以先用它们搭个简单些的壁垒。
t_en: There are shields stored in the vicinity. Let's use them to construct a basic barricade before those two merchants get back.

t_ch: 你和柯库翁将盾牌摆放好。
t_en: You and Glykon go set up the shields.

t_ch: 嗯…效果还可以。等他们带着盾牌和矛回来，就不必担心黑潮的袭击了。
t_en: Hmm... looks good enough. When they come back with shields and lances, we'll be safe from the black tide's intrusion.

t_ch: 希望他们平安归来。
t_en: Hope they make it back in one piece.

t_ch: 别对他们的货抱太大希望。
t_en: Don't expect too much from their merchandise.

t_ch: 我好像听见了什么声音。是不是他们到了？
t_en: I think I heard something. Have they arrived?

t_ch: 你们侧耳倾听，可那并非小贩们的交谈，而是更尖锐、更密集的号叫。
t_en: You listen carefully, but instead of merchant chatter, you hear shrill, intense cries.

t_ch: 黑色乱流沿着街巷汹涌而来，又一次袭击来临了。
t_en: Black turbulence surges violently through the streets. Another attack is upon you.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#泰坦嘶吼}Dilas{RUBY_E#}！
t_en: {RUBY_B#Titan roars}Dilas{RUBY_E#}!

t_ch: 呵呵，不通人性的野兽，你们要是听说过我的名字，一定会望风而逃。
t_en: Hehe, you senseless beasts. If you knew who I am, you'd be running for your lives.

t_ch: 我们会像驾战车一样将它们通通碾碎，对不对，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: We'll crush them all like riding a chariot, right, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 一起上吧，柯库翁！
t_en: Let's do this, Glykon!

t_ch: 呼哧，呼哧…这帮怪物…怎么像追着要签名的崇拜者一样不依不饶！
t_en: Huff, huff... These creatures... chasing me like fans desperate for an autograph!

t_ch: {RUBY_B#泰坦嘶吼}Dilas{RUBY_E#}！
t_en: {RUBY_B#Titan roars}Dilas{RUBY_E#}!

t_ch: 振作起来，柯库翁！
t_en: Pull yourself together, Glykon!

t_ch: 我、我实在打不动了……
t_en: I just... can't go on fighting anymore...

t_ch: 向对面圣殿的守卫呼救。
t_en: Call out to the guards at the temple across the way.

t_ch: 仓促间，你看见对面的守卫也在袭击中苦苦支撑。
t_en: In the chaos, you see the guards there struggling against the attack as well.

t_ch: 黑潮怪物仍源源不断地聚拢过来，眼看即将闯入中庭。正在此时，门外一道声音传来——
t_en: The black tide creatures continue to gather in waves, about to breach the courtyard. Just then, a voice comes from outside—

t_ch: 我们来了！
t_en: We have arrived!

t_ch: 呵呵……
t_en: Hehe...

t_ch: 当黑潮淹没最高的山峰，填平最深的沟壑，能够承载我们最后的希望，开启新的纪元的，唯有它——「最强之盾」！
t_en: When the black tide swallows the tallest mountains and levels the deepest valleys, only one thing can bear our last hope and herald a new era — the Strongest Shield!

t_ch: 当黑夜取代最后的黎明，侵吞一切光芒，能够破除邪恶的威胁，守护整个世界的，唯有它——「最强之矛」！
t_en: When the Night overtakes the last Dawn, devouring all brightness, only one thing can banish evil threats and safeguard the whole world — the Strongest Lance!

t_ch: 放心吧，我们会把它们送到你们的手上！
t_en: Rest assured, we'll deliver them to you!

t_ch: 别做傻事，快跑！
t_en: Run! Don't do anything stupid!

t_ch: 难度是不是有点大啊？
t_en: Are you sure you can do this?

t_ch: 太好了，我们有救了！
t_en: Thank goodness, we're saved!

t_ch: 呵呵……
t_en: Hehe...

t_ch: 曾有客人问我们，如果用「最强之矛」攻击「最强之盾」会发生什么。尝试的结果是，两者都一同化为齑粉。
t_en: A customer once asked what would happen if the Strongest Lance were to strike the Strongest Shield. We tested it — both items pulverized each other into dust.

t_ch: 但是，矛和盾本不该相向。当它们调转方向面向共同的敌人时，又会发生什么呢？
t_en: But lance and shield were never meant to oppose one another. What do you think would happen if they turned together to face a common enemy?

t_ch: ——拭目以待吧！
t_en: Here, watch this!

t_ch: 两位小贩背起行囊，左手持矛，右手持盾，朝中庭冲来——
t_en: The two merchants lift their packs, gripping lances in their left hands and shields in their right, charging into the courtyard—

t_ch: 锋刃击碎长矛，打破盾牌，最后刺入身体。
t_en: A blade shatters lances, breaks shields, and finally pierces flesh.

t_ch: 就这么顶着刀锋和箭矢，他们冲进中庭，摔倒在地。
t_en: Facing blades and arrows, they charge into the courtyard and collapse to the ground.

t_ch: 这就是——
t_en: This...

t_ch: 最强的矛和盾。
t_en: ...is the Strongest Lance and Shield.

t_ch: 两人这么说着，含笑着闭上了眼睛。
t_en: With their last words, they close their eyes with peaceful smiles.

t_ch: 到底在燃些什么啊！
t_en: What on earth are they sacrificing themselves for!?

t_ch: 今天是非死不可吗？
t_en: Do they just want to die so badly?

t_ch: 再创世不是用来任性的啊！
t_en: Era Nova is not meant for recklessness!

t_ch: 啊，啊，啊——
t_en: Ah, oh, mm—

t_ch: 这、这种有点好笑又有点难过的情绪，是怎么回事！
t_en: Wh—What's with this funny yet sad feeling!?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，没时间为他们悲伤了，反正再创世之后又会相见的。我拦住它们，你用他们带来的货物加固壁垒！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, no time to grieve for them now. We'll meet them again in the Era Nova. I'll hold them off while you reinforce the barricade with the goods they brought.

t_ch: 柯库翁拾起小贩行囊中的矛和盾，阻拦住黑潮造物。你也赶忙用它们继续完善壁垒。
t_en: Glykon takes up the lance and shield from the merchants' belongings to hold back the Black Tide Creatures. Meanwhile, you rush to enhance the barricade with the remaining items.

t_ch: 当最后一个黑潮造物被消灭，壁垒也完成了。
t_en: The last of the Black Tide Creatures is vanquished just as the barricade is completed.

t_ch: 啊，是您。我在对面目睹了你们的战斗，真是千钧一发啊！我们也想赶来支援，可惜也被黑潮的袭击牵制住了。
t_en: Ah, it's you. I saw your fight from across the way — that was a close one! We tried to come help, but the black tide had us cornered as well.

t_ch: 现在好了，多亏那两位英雄带来的矛和盾，我们能用更少的人手守住这里了。
t_en: Things are looking up now, thanks to the lance and shield those two heroes delivered. We can now defend this area with a smaller force.

t_ch: 但愿他们能安享轻柔的沉眠。
t_en: May they fall into a pleasant slumber.

t_ch: 关于避难的难民…
t_en: About the refugees...

t_ch: 此前在后厅中避难的公民大部分安然无恙。虽然有些捉襟见肘，但克拉特鲁斯阁下让我们带了些物资和药物过来，伤者已经得到了治疗。
t_en: Most of the citizens sheltering in the back hall are safe. Despite our limited supplies, Lord Krateros sent provisions and medications, and we've treated all the wounded.

t_ch: 也有被困他处的公民前来避难，我们会好好照顾他们，直到新世界来临。
t_en: Some citizens trapped elsewhere have also come seeking shelter. We'll take good care of them until the new world arrives.

t_ch: 关于这些矛和盾…
t_en: About these lances and shields...

t_ch: 呃，说实话，这批军备的质量实在不敢恭维。
t_en: Uh, honestly, the quality of this batch of arms leaves much to be desired.

t_ch: 不过这种紧要关头，能找到它们就已经谢天谢地了。能撑一时是一时吧。
t_en: But in these desperate times, we're thankful to have found them at all. They'll last as long as they can.

t_ch: 没别的事了。
t_en: I have nothing else to ask.

t_ch: 向天父起誓，我们一定全力以赴，不让诸位的努力白费。
t_en: By the sky father, we will do our utmost not to let everyone's efforts go to waste.

t_ch: 啊，是你，{NICKNAME}。咳…我感觉…有点不舒服……
t_en: Ah, it's you, {NICKNAME}. Ahem... I'm not... feeling so great...

t_ch: 放心，你很难死的。
t_en: Relax, you're pretty tough.

t_ch: 给我签一个名吧？
t_en: Could I get your autograph?

t_ch: 哈哈…我…有些没力气了……
t_en: Haha... I don't... have much energy left...

t_ch: 克拉特鲁斯那老顽固，我就说我们能行吧！咳——
t_en: That old stubborn Krateros, I said we could handle it! Ahem—

t_ch: 刚才我们的战斗，是我最精彩的一次登场。也多亏了那两位小贩，没有他们，我们守不住圣殿。
t_en: That battle was my most impressive showing ever. And we also have those two merchants to thank — without them, the temple would have fallen.

t_ch: 他们真的很有信念感。
t_en: They had such convictions.

t_ch: 无论如何，也太莽撞了。
t_en: Regardless, they were too reckless.

t_ch: 恐怕他们只是觉得好玩而已…
t_en: I think they just did it for the thrill...

t_ch: 即便灵魂真的会去往再创世后的世界，但这一刻，死亡带来的恐惧和伤痛仍是真实的。
t_en: Even if souls really do travel to the world after Era Nova, the fear and pain of death at that moment remain real.

t_ch: 不管出于什么原因，也不管用了什么方式…豁出性命，让更多人免于死亡的折磨，都是一件高尚的事。
t_en: Whatever their reasons, whatever their methods... sacrificing one's life to spare others from the torment of death is a noble deed.

t_ch: 但愿再创世后，他们真的能够站上奥赫玛小贩业的顶点。到那时候，你们也一定要去看看「奥凯纳斯运动会」的盛况啊！
t_en: I hope that after Era Nova, they truly rise to become the finest merchants in Okhema. When that happens, you absolutely must witness the grandeur of the Okeanas Games!

t_ch: 商贩汇聚，征战不休的城邦也放下兵戈。
t_en: The merchants convene, and constantly warring city-states pause their conflicts.

t_ch: 街巷、圣殿的廊柱都挂满鲜花，说着不同城邦方言的人们支起帐篷，赞美彼此健美的身体，角逐月桂花环……
t_en: Streets and temple columns are adorned with flowers, people speaking different dialects raise tents, praise each other's athletic bodies, and compete for laurel wreaths...

t_ch: 我要坐第一排。
t_en: I'll take a front-row seat.

t_ch: 别忘了我的致谢词。
t_en: Don't forget to mention me in your speech.

t_ch: 呵呵…一定……
t_en: Hehe... of course...

t_ch: 啊，这不是{NICKNAME}吗。你来探望我们啦？
t_en: Ah, if it isn't {NICKNAME}! Are you here to visit us?

t_ch: 呃，你们不是牺牲了吗？
t_en: Oh, didn't you guys sacrifice yourselves?

t_ch: 卫兵说你们安享沉眠了。
t_en: The guard said that you've fallen into slumber.

t_ch: 卫兵大人的意思是「大家小声点，别吵着两位英雄睡觉」。
t_en: Mr. Guard meant to say, "quieten down, everyone, don't wake those two heroes from their sleep."

t_ch: 哈哈，刚才气氛都到那个份上了，不说点悲壮的话，都有些下不来台了。
t_en: Haha, the atmosphere was so intense back then, it'll be awkward not to say something tragically stirring.

t_ch: 那之后，柯库翁哭天抢地地把医师喊过来，其实只受了点轻伤这件事更是说不出口了。
t_en: After that, Glykon called for a healer, all while in anguish. That made it even harder to say that our injuries weren't severe at all.

t_ch: 你们没事，实在是太好了。
t_en: I'm glad you guys are okay.

t_ch: 下次还是慎重行事些为好。
t_en: It's better to be more cautious next time.

t_ch: 搞笑角色血条一般都很长。
t_en: Comedic relief characters tend to live longer.

t_ch: 医师帮我们包扎了伤口，嘱咐我们好好休息。我的胳膊上挨了两剑，千万别留下病根啊，我还指望着用它搬货呢。
t_en: The healer helped to dress our wounds and instructed us to get plenty of rest. I was cut by a blade twice in the arm, and I really hope that doesn't leave any lasting side effects. I still need it to move goods.

t_ch: 我是膝盖上中了一剑…下回摆摊被卫兵大人撵，会不会影响逃跑的速度啊？
t_en: I took a blade to the knee... Will it affect how quickly I can escape the next time Mr. Guard chases after me for peddling?

t_ch: 怎么还想着卖货呢？
t_en: Why are you still thinking about selling your goods?

t_ch: 要适应英雄的身份。
t_en: You have to adapt to your role as a hero.

t_ch: 哼哼，这点小插曲， 不会动摇我们的决心。
t_en: Heheh, that's merely a trifle. It won't sway us.

t_ch: 毕竟，我们是要站上奥赫玛小贩业顶点的男人啊！
t_en: After all, we're the men who will stand at the pinnacle of the Okhema marketplace together!

t_ch: 这么一来，我们的「最强之矛」和「最强之盾」也算打响名头，销路不愁了吧！
t_en: With that, we've made a name for ourselves as "the Strongest Lance" and "the Strongest Shield"! That'll surely boost our sales!

t_ch: 言之有理！
t_en: You're right!

t_ch: 这个老顽固……
t_en: This stubborn old guy...

t_ch: 我记得这边有一些盾牌，把它们扛上！
t_en: I remember there are shields over here, grab them!

t_ch: 不错，够结实。把它放在门口吧。
t_en: Hmm, they seem sturdy enough. Put it by the doorway.

t_ch: 一时疏忽，你还未抵达廊下便被怪物撵上。好在你将身一扭，躲过了袭击！
t_en: With a momentary lapse, the creatures catch up to you before you reach the corridor. Fortunately, with a quick twist, you dodge their attack!

t_ch: 必须把怪物引到二楼廊道下方，再试试看吧。
t_en: I need to lure the creatures to the area beneath the second-floor corridor. Let's try again.

t_ch: 这边的也别忘了。
t_en: Don't forget this one too.

t_ch: 这里也有盾牌，捎上吧。
t_en: There's a shield here too. Stack it up as well.

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}，你来得刚好，能帮我作个证吗？他们都不相信我说的话。
t_en: {F#Young madam}{M#Young sir}, perfect timing! Can you vouch for me? They don't believe what I said.

t_ch: 作证？你又做什么坏事啦？
t_en: Vouch for what? What've you nicked now?

t_ch: 放心放心，我再也没干过小偷小摸的事了。
t_en: Cross my heart! No more sticky fingers, promise!

t_ch: 自从接受了刻法勒的信仰，我现在是全新的希瑞雅了。每天晚上我都在梦里重游云崖，一遍遍观览那些好大好大的壁画。
t_en: Since finding Kephale's light, I've become a brand-new me! Every night I spirit-walk Dawncloud, memorizing those cathedral-sized murals.

t_ch: 等等，你是…？
t_en: Wait... you are...?

t_ch: 是我呀！希瑞雅。我…偷过你的钱袋，还编过一些故事，撒过一些小谎……
t_en: It's me, Serena! Your former purse-liberator? Occasional fibber?

t_ch: 不过嘛，我现在天天都在做好事，期盼着到了再创世，我能成为一名真正的刻法勒祭司！
t_en: But now? Daily do-gooder! In the Era Nova, I'll be a proper priestess of Kephale!

t_ch: 可是，这两个小鬼，竟然敢说…刻法勒的庇佑会失去效力，圣城里的大家只能白白等死。真是气死我了！
t_en: But these ankle-biters claim... Kephale's shield's failing. That we're sitting ducks here in the holy city. I cannot bear it anymore!

t_ch: 这位{F#女士}{M#先生}，你来得刚好，听说你是黄金裔大人们的贵客，请你告诉他们两个——
t_en: {F#Ma'am}{M#Sir}, you got here at the perfect time. I've heard that you're the distinguished guest of the Chrysos Heirs! Tell these two:

t_ch: 虽然奥赫玛现在面临危机，但天父刻法勒还在庇护着我们所有人，是这样的，对吗？
t_en: Okhema's struggling, but Sky Father Kephale's still got us. Yeah?

t_ch: 你是？
t_en: And you are?

t_ch: 我叫希瑞雅，来自一个名叫阿卡索斯的城邦。阿卡索斯它…已经被黑潮淹没了。但我相信，奥赫玛一定不会重蹈覆辙！
t_en: Serena of Icatus. My home... consumed by the black tide now. But Okhema? Different story!

t_ch: 孩子们，怎么不去避难？
t_en: Kids! Why aren't you at the shelters?

t_ch: 没救了，奥赫玛已经没救了。比起被怪物杀死，不如让我被陨石砸死算了。
t_en: We're doomed. Okhema's doomed. But I'd rather get crushed by meteors than killed by monsters.

t_ch: 说什么呢，亚森！不许垂头丧气的，我们脚下站着的地方明明很安全。
t_en: Yason! No giving up! We're standing on safe ground. Obviously!

t_ch: 没错，赞美仁慈的父！
t_en: Aye! Praise the merciful Father!

t_ch: 赞美仁慈的父！瞧，这位{F#女士}{M#先生}的担保和我说的一样吧？
t_en: Praise the merciful Father! See? the {F#young madam}{M#young sir} agrees! Told you!

t_ch: 可是，这两个小鬼，竟然敢说…刻法勒的庇佑会失去效力，圣城里的大家只能白白等死。真是气死我了！
t_en: But these ankle-biters claim... Kephale's shield's failing. That we're sitting ducks here in the holy city. I cannot bear it anymore!

t_ch: 希瑞雅，你说你在黎明云崖听到了天父的回应，你能再说一遍回应的内容吗？
t_en: Serena, you said you heard the sky father's response at Dawncloud. Can you tell us what they said again?

t_ch: 当然！这些天来，我一直背诵着，一字不差。
t_en: Of course! I've spent the last couple of days inscribing every word into my mind.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「——我会为你们背负这沉重的黎明，将漆黑的凶潮隔绝于圣城之外——」</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>"— I will carry the burden of this heavy dawn, shielding the Holy City from the tides of darkness..."</color>

t_ch: 是先前在神山上，天父的声音清清楚楚告诉我的。
t_en: Clear as day — the sky father's voice on the peak of the holy mountain.

t_ch: 可我们也看得清清楚楚，它肩负的「黎明」已经不再亮着了啊。
t_en: But we can clearly see that the "Dawn" on their shoulders isn't shining anymore.

t_ch: 要么是天父的神谕出了错……
t_en: Either the sky father made a mistake...

t_ch: 不可能！神谕绝对不会错的。
t_en: Blasphemy! The prophecy can't be wrong.

t_ch: 那就是——希瑞雅，你其实根本没有去过黎明云崖，都是你编出来骗我们的吧？
t_en: Then... Serena, you've never actually been to Dawncloud, have you? It's all a bunch of lies you made up to fool us?

t_ch: 坏了，真成小赛飞儿了。
t_en: Oh no... We have a mini-Cipher here.

t_ch: 神谕确实有假的。
t_en: The prophecy does indeed have falsehoods.

t_ch: 别吵架啊，有话好好说。
t_en: Please don't resort to violence. We can talk things through.

t_ch: 希瑞雅，没必要为大家做到这个地步。谎话说太多，到了新世界你就当不上刻法勒的祭司了。反正我也做好了去见塞纳托斯的准备……
t_en: Serena, there's no need for altruism. Lies will only dash your hopes of becoming a priestess of Kephale in the new world. I'm ready for Thanatos' embrace...

t_ch: 呸呸呸！你不准说了。你们两个…是不是非要亲耳听到才肯相信？
t_en: Hey! Stop talking! You two... Do you need to hear it yourselves to believe it?

t_ch: 那我，那我……
t_en: Then I... I...

t_ch: 唉！要是能找到上次放我进云崖的好心守卫叔叔，偷偷和他打个招呼就好了。只是，那次已经够麻烦他了，我不想影响到他的工作。
t_en: Ugh! If only we can find that nice guard who smuggled me into Dawncloud last time... I would like to sneakily say hi to him. Just... I think we troubled him enough last time. I don't want to disturb his work.

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}，我恳求你，能和云崖的其他守卫通融一下，带上我、亚森、拉缇娜我们三个，去到刻法勒的巨像前，再祈祷一回吗？
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, I'm begging you. Can you come to some kind of agreement with the other Dawncloud guards and bring me, Yason, Lathena to perform one last prayer at the Colossus of Kephale?

t_ch: 我想让他们知道，刻法勒的声音有多么坚定、温暖，奥赫玛绝对不会被黑潮攻陷，背负世界的泰坦从没有忘记过我们这些平民。
t_en: Let them feel the determination and warmth in Kephale's voice, let them be convinced that Okhema will stand tall against the black tide, and the Titan who bears the world has never forgotten about us.

t_ch: 一带三？太难为我了。
t_en: Three stowaways? You're really going to do this to me?

t_ch: 呜！没关系，我还有办法，一定还会有别的办法的。
t_en: Hmph! Fine. I got some other plans. There's got to be some other ideas we can use.

t_ch: 没问题，我帮你问问守卫。
t_en: Sure. I'll ask the guard for you.

t_ch: 咦？真的可以？
t_en: Huh... really?

t_ch: 太好啦！要是能再去一趟，我一定会看好他们，快去快回，绝不到处乱跑。
t_en: Great! If we make another trip, I'll definitely keep a close eye on them. We get in and get out, no detours at all.

t_ch: 那我们就在这里乖乖等着，等你的好消息。
t_en: We will be waiting here for your good news.

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}，你又回来啦？
t_en: {F#Young madam}{M#Young sir}, you're back!

t_ch: 我想让亚森和拉缇娜也能去到黎明云崖，聆听到刻法勒的神谕，可以拜托你和云崖的守卫说说情吗？
t_en: I want Yason and Lathena to hear Kephale's prophecy at Dawncloud. Could you please sweet-talk the guards?

t_ch: 我考虑过了，还是不行。
t_en: Thought about it. Still nope.

t_ch: 呜！没关系，我还有办法，一定还会有别的办法的。
t_en: Hmph! Fine. I got some other plans. There's got to be some other ideas we can use.

t_ch: 没问题，我帮你问问守卫。
t_en: Sure. I'll ask the guard for you.

t_ch: 太好啦！那我们就在这里乖乖等着，等你的好消息。
t_en: Great! We will be waiting here for your good news.

t_ch: 你当然知道，所谓「刻法勒」的回应，是女孩口中「好心的守卫叔叔」假扮的。
t_en: As you know, the supposed response from "Kephale" was done by "Mr. Nice Guard," who the girl told you about.

t_ch: 一个谎言需要无数个谎言来圆…要实现孩子们的心愿，恐怕需要再次找到这个守卫，和他事先串通好。
t_en: A lie needs to be helped up by infinitely more lies... Looks like you gotta find the guard again and deal with him in advance.

t_ch: 回想他的长相。
t_en: Recall his face.

t_ch: 他有着一张奥赫玛最常见、最普通的面孔，和千千万万个圣城守卫一样。你很难在人群中找到他。
t_en: He's got that extremely common and very ordinary face for an Okheman, just like all the countless other guards in the holy city. You'll be hard-pressed to find him in a crowd.

t_ch: 回想他的名字。
t_en: Recall his name.

t_ch: 你们接受了他的帮助，却忘记了问一句这位好心人的名字。
t_en: You accepted his help but forgot to ask this nice guy his name.

t_ch: 想不起来了…先去黎明云崖转一圈看看吧。
t_en: I'm drawing blanks... Let's case Dawncloud first.

t_ch: 你知道，刻法勒已经不再言语很久，女孩听到的回应没有那么简单，这个忙帮起来势必有点麻烦。
t_en: You know, Kephale's been silent for ages. The reply the girl hears shouldn't be that simple. This bit of aid might turn into quite the hassle.

t_ch: 但孩童诚挚的目光让人无法拒绝，你计划先找到她口中那位「好心的守卫叔叔」，打听一下消息。
t_en: But the sincere gaze of a child is a hard thing to resist. You plan to find that "Mr. Nice Guard" she talked about first and get some details.

t_ch: 去黎明云崖转一圈看看吧。
t_en: Let's case Dawncloud first.

t_ch: 站住，你是——哦！是 {NICKNAME}阁下。
t_en: Stop! You... Oh! It's you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 战火已经烧到了奥赫玛。阁下虽然武艺高强，但若没有要紧的事，还是别在外面闲逛了，速去避难吧！
t_en: The war's already reached Okhema. You might be pretty skilled and powerful, but if you don't have anything urgent that's keeping you out here, it's best not to linger outside and find shelter!

t_ch: 这名守卫的语气和他的盔甲一样坚硬，看起来并不是会轻易心软的类型。
t_en: The guard's tone is as tough as his armor. He doesn't seem like the type to go tender easily.

t_ch: 告诉他你在找人。
t_en: Mention you're looking for someone.

t_ch: 呃，一位「好心的守卫叔叔」？还有什么别的信息吗？
t_en: Uh, someone called "Mr. Nice Guard"? You got any other details?

t_ch: （再多的信息就暴露了，希瑞雅不想给那位守卫带来麻烦……）
t_en: (If you give any more details, you'll expose things. Serena doesn't want to cause that guard more trouble...)

t_ch: 告诉他孩子们的请求。
t_en: Tell him of the children's request.

t_ch: 不，这可不行！现在都乱成什么样了，孩子们就该好好待在安全的地方。
t_en: What? Absolutely not! Look at the sorry state of things, the kids should stay where they're safe.

t_ch: 找个话题试探一下。
t_en: Find a topic to use as a probe for information.

t_ch: 您还有什么事吗？
t_en: Do you need something else?

t_ch: 咳，你看着很面生啊！
t_en: Ahem. You don't look like you're from around here!

t_ch: 今天执守的卫兵换人了吗？
t_en: Did today's guard rotation get changed?

t_ch: 阁下眼熟的或许是我的同僚吧？十多年来我们一直轮换值守同一班岗位。
t_en: The one you're familiar with might be my colleague, maybe? We've been on the cycling in and out on the same post for more than a decade.

t_ch: 但现在情况危急，圣城卫队抽调了一大批人去抵抗黑潮，这里只留下了小部分卫兵负责维护治安、疏散公民。
t_en: But the situation is critical. The holy city guards transferred a bunch of people to hold back the tide. There's only a small group left here to maintain order and evacuate the civilians.

t_ch: 我的同僚，他也被调去了前线…哎！我本想和他交换一下，他却怎么也不肯，只是让我安心留在这里，说前线的事情交给他就好。
t_en: My colleague got deployed to frontline... I'd switch with him, but he refused. He just told me to stay here and leave the frontlines to him.

t_ch: 我知道，他父亲也是一名圣城卫兵，杀死过黑潮怪物…他一直想做同样的事，好证明自己也有能力保护家人与奥赫玛。
t_en: I know. His father was a guard of the city too, slaying monsters of the black tide... He always wanted to do the same thing, to prove he had the strength to protect his family and Okhema.

t_ch: 圣城有你，才是真的了不起。
t_en: The holy city's only holy because it's got you in it.

t_ch: 汗流浃背了，阁下夸他就好，我也没做什么。
t_en: Heh. You got me acting up a cold sweat here. You can just praise my friend. I didn't do much.

t_ch: 他去了哪里作战呢？
t_en: Where's he stationed?

t_ch: 从刻法勒广场到圣城门口那段，是抵御黑潮入侵的重要防线。这批卫兵应该都被派驻在那里了。
t_en: From Kephale Plaza to the city gates — the main black tide defense line. This batch of guards might all be deployed there.

t_ch: 真希望他们平安归来，这回我无论如何也要跟他换个班。
t_en: Hope they make it back in one piece. This time, I'm swapping shifts with him no matter what.

t_ch: （去他说的地方找找吧，说不定还能帮上卫兵们的忙……）
t_en: (Go to the place he talked about. Maybe you can lend the guards a hand even...)

t_ch: 年轻的卫兵倒在地上，肢体已然僵硬，无法再挥舞起哪怕是最轻的一把武器。
t_en: A young guard lies stiff on the ground, his limbs completely rigid, never to lift even the lightest weapon ever again.

t_ch: 他身上没有任何铭牌和遗物，无从辨认生前的身份。你叹了口气，为他阖上了眼皮。
t_en: No nameplates or relics. Nothing to even identify him with. You sigh, closing his eyelids.

t_ch: 这名卫兵盔甲下的身体遭到了黑潮的侵蚀，在漆黑的死灰蔓延至心脏的位置前，他因负伤过重停止了呼吸。
t_en: The guard's body beneath his armor has sustained black tide corruption. Even before the darkness spread to his heart, he passed from the severity of his injuries.

t_ch: 没有被黑潮彻底侵吞理智，沦为怪物，或许是命运对他最后的仁慈。
t_en: Not being completely consumed of rationality by the black tide and turned into a monstrosity might be fate's final mercy for him.

t_ch: 你抱着最坏的预感试探了他的鼻息，果然已无生命迹象。他胸口的铭牌上刻着几个模糊的字：斐…第…斯？
t_en: You check his breathing with the worst case scenario in mind and confirm the absence of life in his body. His nameplate on his chest has a few blurred words inscribed there: Phe...d...es?

t_ch: 「呲啦——」一声，有什么动静从它的盔甲下传出。你仔细检查，发现源头是一枚石制的扩声器。
t_en: "Click..." A sound comes. A disturbance resounds from beneath his armor. You check carefully, and discover the source to be a stone-made voice amplifier.

t_ch: 斐第斯，还在摆弄你那个扩声器吗？我记得那是你父亲的遗物？
t_en: Phedes, still fiddling with that amplifier? I remember that was something that belonged to your father.

t_ch: 是啊…我小时候，他最喜欢用扩声器假扮各种声音逗我玩。
t_en: Yeah... He liked to use it to play pretend... making voices for me when I was small.

t_ch: 父亲战死后，我一直收藏着它，本以为再也派不上用场了。想不到我也有故意用它来捏造假声的时候，那天在黎明云崖上……
t_en: After he died in battle, I kept it. I thought it would never come in handy again, but it turns out I ended up mimicking voices with it sometimes too... And that day, at Dawncloud...

t_ch: 虽说是个情急之下的意外，但看那孩子开心的模样，我觉得这谎撒得很值。真希望她现在还相信着啊！
t_en: It was just an unexpected moment born of desperation, but seeing how happy it made the kid, I thought it was a worthwhile lie. I really hope she's still keeping the faith now!

t_ch: 是嘛？你假扮了谁？
t_en: Really? Who did you impersonate?

t_ch: 嗯…如果我说，我假扮了刻法勒，你信不信？
t_en: Hm... If I said I impersonated Kephale, would you believe me?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，别逗了，你是不是看现在战事紧张，故意说笑话让我们心情轻松轻松。
t_en: Ha! Stop joking around. Say, you're not deliberately jesting to lift our moods with how desperately the war's going, right?

t_ch: ——糟了，看那边，黑潮怪物又来了！
t_en: Oh no! Look there! Black tide monsters incoming!

t_ch: 这是第几波了…走吧，准备作战。可不能让圣城的孩子们失去笑容啊！
t_en: Which wave of battle are we on now... Let's go. Let's get ready. We can't let the kids of the holy city lose their smiles!

t_ch: 是这么回事啊…我明白了。
t_en: So that's how it was... I understand.

t_ch: 斐第斯，斐第斯，放我们进入负世祭坛，又假扮刻法勒回应希瑞雅的「好心的守卫叔叔」，原来是叫这个名字。
t_en: Phedes, Phedes... Oh, "Mr. Nice Guard" who sneaked us into the Worldbearing Altar and impersonated Kephale for Serena — that was his name.

t_ch: 可惜，他已经牺牲了啊……
t_en: But, sadly, he fell...

t_ch: 是这么回事啊…我好像猜到了。
t_en: So that's how it was... I think I guessed it right.

t_ch: 希瑞雅听到的「刻法勒」的回应，是这个叫斐第斯的守卫用扩声器假扮的。恐怕他就是那个「好心的守卫叔叔」。
t_en: The "Kephale" voice Serena heard was impersonated using Phedes' amplifier. It unfortunately appears that you just found "Mr. Nice Guard."

t_ch: 但是，他已经牺牲了啊……
t_en: But he lost his life ...

t_ch: 沉思之际，一阵绝望的悲泣从旁边传来……
t_en: As you sink deep into your thoughts, a despairing wail comes off from the side...

t_ch: 全知伟大的天父啊，看看这些可怜的卫兵，看看你的子民吧！为什么永恒的圣城会出现死难者，难道你抛弃他们了吗？
t_en: All-knowing sky father! See your fallen guards, your people! Why does death stain the holy city? Have you abandoned them?

t_ch: 再创世…我们的再创世，我一直笃信，我们所有人都会在安谧的梦乡中迎来那片温暖、光明的未来。
t_en: The Era Nova... Our Era Nova... I always believed that we would awaken to that warm, radiant future in tranquil dreams.

t_ch: 可是，为什么你背负的黎明机器不再发出光芒了？大司铎大人不是说它会照拂圣城…直到永远吗？
t_en: But why does your Dawn Device darken? Didn't the chaplain say it would protect us till... forever?

t_ch: 祭司的悲泣声渐弱，你心知「照拂圣城，直到永远」是赛飞儿的谎言。谎言被戳破、信仰崩塌带来的后果，希瑞雅能接受吗？
t_en: The priest's sobs fade. You know the "eternal protection" was a lie that Cipher's created. When truth is revealed and faith shatters, will Serena be able to handle the following consequences?

t_ch: 不能让孩子们失去笑容！
t_en: We can't let the kids lose their smiles!

t_ch: 是啊，你想起了斐第斯留下的最后一句话——「可不能让圣城的孩子们失去笑容啊！」
t_en: Yes, indeed. You recall Phedes' last words — "We can't let the kids of the holy city lose their smiles!"

t_ch: 但…究竟该怎么做呢？
t_en: But... How should we proceed?

t_ch: 一个计划从你心中浮现：捡起斐第斯的石制扩声器，把事情的原委告诉那位正直的守卫，恳请他带孩子们来到刻法勒的神像前……
t_en: A plan forms in your mind: You take Phedes' stone-made amplifier and tell that upright guard the whole story, asking him to bring the children before the colossus...

t_ch: 然后用你的谎言，亲自完成孩子们、也是斐第斯最后的心愿。
t_en: And then you use your lies to complete the children and Phedes's final wish.

t_ch: 就这么办！
t_en: Let's do that then!

t_ch: 第二天，门扉时分，你事先清扫了云崖上徘徊的怪物，来到了刻法勒的神像前……
t_en: The next day, after clearing the monsters roaming Dawncloud at Entry Hour, you stand before Kephale's statue...

t_ch: 时间刚刚好，希瑞雅他们还没到，我先去后面藏起来。
t_en: Perfect timing. I'll hide before Serena and them arrive.

t_ch: 站在这里，身形足够隐蔽，你摸了摸口袋里那枚石制的扩声器，想象着「好心的守卫叔叔」藏身在此回应希瑞雅的场景，做好了准备。
t_en: Standing there, you're well concealed enough. You grip the stone amplifier in your pocket and think of how "Mr. Nice Guard" responded to Serena hiding here. Then, you prepared yourself.

t_ch: 一阵脚步声传来，在守卫的看护下，希瑞雅、亚森、拉缇娜三人来到了巨像前……
t_en: Footsteps approach. Under a guard escort, Serena, Yason, and Lathena arrive and stand before the Colossus...

t_ch: …孩子们，出于 {NICKNAME}阁下的恳请，这回破例带你们来黎明云崖，观仰泰坦的神体。
t_en: ...Kids, by {NICKNAME}'s sincerest request, we will make an exception and allow you into Dawncloud to behold the Titan's sacred form.

t_ch: 任意外出原本是非常危险的，我本来计划遇到怪物就回去，好在——感谢刻法勒的庇护，一路上我们都没有碰到怪物。
t_en: It's extremely dangerous to wander out recklessly, so I intended for us to return the moment we encounter any monsters. But thanks to Kephale's divine providence, we didn't cross paths with any.

t_ch: （咳咳，是啊…幸亏我路上先清扫了一遍……）
t_en: (Ahem... Yeah... Thankfully I cleared out the roads first...)

t_ch: 抓紧时间吧，你们可以每人向泰坦祈祷一会儿。
t_en: Don't waste time. You can all pray to the Titan for a while.

t_ch: 谢谢叔叔！好壮观啊，希瑞雅，原来你真来过黎明云崖？
t_en: Thanks, mister! It's an amazing sight! Serena, you really came here before?

t_ch: 都说了我没骗人，你们祈祷吧！先说好，要虔诚，心怀信仰…天父一定会像当时回应我那样，认真地回应你们的心愿。对吧，守卫叔叔？
t_en: Told you I wasn't lying! Go ahead and pray now! Oh, and you have to do it with piety and devoted faith... The sky father will definitely respond to your wishes as genuinely as he did mine. Ain't that right, Mr. Guard?

t_ch: 啊？对的，对的对的。
t_en: Ah? Yes, of course.

t_ch: 黎明机器…好大，好近，它会不会从天上砸下来？砸中我们？
t_en: Dawn Machine... So big. Will it fall on us?

t_ch: 保持沉默。
t_en: Remain silent.

t_ch: 奇怪，怎么没有回应？
t_en: Weird, no response?

t_ch: 刻法勒也不想回答这种，呃，有点蠢的问题吧。
t_en: Kephale won't answer such... stupid questions.

t_ch: 小心哦！泰坦举久了也会累。
t_en: Careful! Titans get tired too.

t_ch: 「——人子啊，黎明机器的确沉重，饶是我的肩头也感到负累，但放心，在你们看不到的地方，我还有一只手在稳稳地托着它——」
t_en: "—Children of humanity, the Dawn Device indeed weighs heavy, even my shoulders. But worry not, for where you cannot see, I still have a hand steadily bearing its weight—"

t_ch: 不会的，法律禁止高空抛物。
t_en: Nah. It's against the law to litter from on high.

t_ch: 「——无需担忧，我曾和律法之泰坦立下誓约：我拥有庇佑世人、承载此世全部记忆的权能，代价则是背负世上最沉重的事物，在有人接过权能之前不得脱手——」
t_en: "—Worry not, I swore with Law Titan: I shall protect all of humanity and wield the authority to every memory in this world. The price will be to bear the heaviest burden until another takes the mantle—"

t_ch: 笨蛋亚森！什么问题啊，白白浪费了一次祈祷的机会，换我来。
t_en: Dummy Yason! What kind of question is that? You've wasted a chance to pray! My turn.

t_ch: 刻法勒，我们的天父，我诚心向你祷问。现在的奥赫玛，万帷网变慢了，消息真真假假，好像到处都在打仗，乱糟糟的，那些山崖上，还冒出了一些奇怪的虚影……
t_en: Sky Father Kephale, I ask you sincerely: Okhema and the World Wound Web have slowed. Be the news true or false, but everywhere seems to be at war, chaos reigns, and on the cliffs, stand strange shadows...

t_ch: 大家都说这是因为再创世要来了，我想知道，您要怎么庇护我们？
t_en: Everyone said that's because Era Nova is coming. What I want to know is: How will you protect us?

t_ch: 会伸出手把我们接应到新世界吗？还是说，在我们死了很久以后，吹一口气让我们重新复活？我很害怕死，我、我希望是前面那种……
t_en: Will you reach out and take us to the new world? Or it's like, after we've been dead for a long time, you'll breathe a breath and resurrect us? I'm very scared of dying, so I—I hope it is the first...

t_ch: 保持沉默。
t_en: Remain silent.

t_ch: 希瑞雅，你听到什么了吗？我怎么一点儿声音也没听到。
t_en: Serena, hear anything? Silence here.

t_ch: 耐心等等嘛！天父又不是卖报的报童，要到处大声吆喝。
t_en: Be patient! sky father's not a town crier, loudly screeching their cries.

t_ch: 别怕，死亡也只是沉眠。
t_en: Don't fear death. It's just sleep.

t_ch: 「——我曾造访过塞纳托斯的冥府，那是一片宁静的花海，所有灵魂都会化作花种，得到死亡泰坦的温柔照拂——」
t_en: "—I visited Thanatos's netherworld. There was a sea of tranquil flowers, where all souls will become floral seeds to receive the care and light of the Death Titan—"

t_ch: 「——别惧怕死亡，率先离去的死者，只是提前一步去了花海——」
t_en: "—Fear not death. The ones who departed earlier are but early arrivals at the sea of flowers—"

t_ch: 「——孩子们，不妨把眼前的灾难视作一场梦境，等噩梦醒来，无论生者还是死者，都会在新世界重逢——」
t_en: "—Treat this disaster as a nightmare, children. When you wake, all will reunite in the new world, be they living or dead—"

t_ch: 都来站我身上，站得下。
t_en: Come, all of you, stand upon me. There is space.

t_ch: 「——咳，我生有伟岸的身躯，是为了在末日的灾厄来临、世界坍塌时，把我创造的人子们接引到我的双手和肩背上，一同带往再创世——」
t_en: "—Ah, I was born with a mighty body, so that when the apocalypse comes and the world collapses, I can carry the children I created with my arms and on my shoulders, so that we might march toward Era Nova together—"

t_ch: 「——你们只需要暂且藏身在安全之处，我许诺：黑潮绝不会侵没圣城，如同我的身躯永远坚屹在此——」
t_en: "—So long as you find a safe place to hide, I promise this: The black tide won't breach the holy city, just as my body will stand here eternal—"

t_ch: 好了，孩子们，祈祷的时间结束了，记住天父赐给你们的回应了吗？
t_en: Alright, children, prayer time's over. Remember how the sky father replied to you?

t_ch: 嗯！陨石和黎明机器都不会砸中我。
t_en: Yeah! Neither meteors nor the Dawn Device will fall on me.

t_ch: 奥赫玛的大家，也都会好好地迎来再创世。
t_en: Everyone in Okhema will definitely reach the Era Nova safely.

t_ch: 不错，聆听过天父回应的孩子，是被选中的人，回去和你们的家人待在一起，安心躲过末日吧！
t_en: Good. Now you're all the chosen children who heard from the sky father. You should be with your families and stay safe and at ease during the apocalypse.

t_ch: 看不到我，看不到我……
t_en: I'm invisible... I'm invisible...

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}，我看到你了！
t_en: {F#Young madam}{M#Young sir}, I see you!

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}，刚才回应我们的声音，是你吧？
t_en: {F#Young madam}{M#Young sir}, it was you who responded to our voices just now, right?

t_ch: 开什么玩笑呢？是刻法勒。
t_en: Are you kidding? It was Kephale.

t_ch: 真的吗…我们现在再祈祷一次，天父还会回应我们吗？
t_en: Really... If we pray again now, will the sky father still respond to us?

t_ch: 抱歉，被识破了，是我。
t_en: Alright. You saw right through it. It was me.

t_ch: 果然，我从前骗过{F#女士}{M#先生}你，这回却被你「骗」了一回呀！
t_en: Figures. I deceived you before, {F#miss}{M#mister}, but now you "tricked" me!

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}，能对我说实话吗？我什么都能接受。上次回应我的声音，也不是真正的刻法勒，是上次那位好心的守卫叔叔吧！
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, tell me the truth. I can handle that. Was that voice last time also fake? It wasn't really Kephale either, right? It was Mr. Nice Guard, right?

t_ch: …你知道？
t_en: ...You knew?

t_ch: 扩声器漏音了？
t_en: The voice amplifier malfunctioned?

t_ch: 都瞒不过你啊，小机灵鬼…
t_en: You're hard to fool, you witty thing...

t_ch: 其实…刻法勒的声音是怎么回事，还有好心的守卫叔叔是谁，我全都知道。不太会骗人的人…瞒不了别人太久。
t_en: Actually... I know about Kephale's voice, who Mr. Nice Guard is, all of it. Bad liars... can't really keep the lie for very long.

t_ch: 我…大家为我，做了这么多事情。一直保护着我们的，不光是遥远的泰坦，还有身边的人。
t_en: I... Everyone did so much for me. The ones who protected me aren't just the distant Titan, but also those around me.

t_ch: 那你，却又来祈祷了一次…？
t_en: Then why did you come to pray again?

t_ch: 嗯，毕竟亚森和拉缇娜他们还不知道嘛！
t_en: Yeah, because Yason and Lathena still don't know yet!

t_ch: 我受过那么多人的照顾，也想照顾照顾比我小的两个小鬼。
t_en: So many people have looked after me, so I wanted to look after two kids who were even smaller than me too.

t_ch: 记得第一次听到刻法勒的回应时，我激动地快要哭出来。如果他们两个能听到，一定也会相信——神谕中的再创世会到来，我们都会得到负世泰坦的庇护。
t_en: I almost burst into tears the first time I heard Kephale's voice. I want them to hear it, too, so that they believe. So that they know when the Era Nova in the prophecy comes, we'll be protected by the Worldbearing Titan.

t_ch: 还有，我可是希瑞雅啊！睡过破木箱、靠偷东西把自己养活的希瑞雅，不管神谕会不会实现，我一直都很想努力地活下去。
t_en: Plus, I'm Serena! I've slept in busted wooden boxes, and kept myself alive by stealing. Don't matter if the prophecy comes true or not, I've always done my best to keep surviving.

t_ch: 回到云石市集后，我还是会告诉所有的大人和孩子们：刻法勒的回应是真的，天父要我们抱着希望，直到末日的最后一天…然后，大家会再次重逢。
t_en: Back at the Marmoreal Market, I'll tell all the adults and kids: Kephale's reply is true, the sky father wants us to hold on to hope, till the last day of the apocalypse... And then, we will all meet again.

t_ch: 希瑞雅，长大了啊。
t_en: Serena, you've grown up.

t_ch: 记得别说漏嘴咯！
t_en: Don't spill the beans!

t_ch: 谢谢你{F#女士}{M#先生}，相信我们也会在「再创世」再相见的！
t_en: Thank you, {F#young madam}{M#young sir}. We'll meet again in the Era Nova!

t_ch: 嗯，我带你回安全的地方吧！
t_en: Well, I'll escort you back.

t_ch: 让陨石把我砸死好了！
t_en: Let meteors crush me!

t_ch: 天父根本不会庇护我们…
t_en: The sky father abandoned us...

t_ch: 都不许胡说！
t_en: Stop that nonsense!

t_ch: 我也有机会听到神谕了？
t_en: I got a chance to hear the prophecy too?

t_ch: 刻法勒的声音，会像老爷爷吧…
t_en: Maybe Kephale sounds like an old grandpa...

t_ch: 这次要问些什么好呢？
t_en: What should I ask this time?

t_ch: 这里…刚刚发生过一场战斗？
t_en: Did a battle just happen here...

t_ch: 结束了，呃，希瑞雅…竟然还没走？悄悄躲着她离开吧！
t_en: It's over. Huh? Serena... is still here? Better sneak away from her quietly!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我、我有一个不情之请……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I... I have a rather awkward request...

t_ch: 我给阿波罗尼写了一封信，可以拜托你帮、帮我送给他吗？
t_en: I wrote a letter to Apollonius, c—could you help me deliver it to him?

t_ch: 有瓜？你好，我吃一口。
t_en: Got some tea? Hey, spill it!

t_ch: 是、是送信，不是送瓜……
t_en: I—It's delivering letter, not tea...

t_ch: 你打算向他表白？
t_en: Are you planning to confess to him?

t_ch: 啊，阁下，不要说、说得那么直接啊……
t_en: Ah, {NICKNAME}, p—please don't be so... so direct...

t_ch: 嗯…个中缘由，{NICKNAME}说、说不定已经猜出来了。
t_en: Hmm... you m—might have already figured out the reason why.

t_ch: 不知道您愿不愿意帮我这个忙呢……
t_en: I was wondering if you'd be willing to help me out...

t_ch: 当然可以，举手之劳。
t_en: No problem, it's a piece of cake.

t_ch: 真、真的吗！太谢、谢谢你了！
t_en: R—Really!? Th—Thank you so much!

t_ch: 给，这是我的信，辛苦{NICKNAME}啦。
t_en: Here's my letter. Thanks for your help, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 但、但不许看！不然，我、我真的会找个地缝钻进去，再也不出来了……
t_en: B—But don't look! Otherwise, I—I'll seriously find a crack in the ground to crawl into and never come out again...

t_ch: 我不擅长应付这种，找别人吧。
t_en: I'm not good at this kind of stuff, ask someone else.

t_ch: 啊，那、那好吧，打扰{NICKNAME}了……
t_en: Oh, w—well then, sorry to bother you, {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: …啊，{NICKNAME}！你是回、回心转意了吗？
t_en: ...Ah, {NICKNAME}! H—Have you had a change of heart?

t_ch: 吟风爵，我果然想吃瓜。
t_en: Dux Carminum, I'm dying to hear the tea.

t_ch: 真、真的吗！太谢、谢谢你了！
t_en: R—Really!? Th—Thank you so much!

t_ch: 我不擅长应付这种，找别人吧。
t_en: I'm not good at this kind of stuff, ask someone else.

t_ch: 啊，那、那好吧，打扰{NICKNAME}了……
t_en: Oh, w—well then, sorry to bother you, {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: （这封信…想必就是维吉妮娅送给阿波罗尼的情书了。）
t_en: (This... must be the love letter Verginia wrote to Apollonius.)

t_ch: 你举着信件，在人群中寻找着阿波罗尼的身影，忽然有人拍了拍你的肩膀。
t_en: Holding the letter, you search for Apollonius in the crowd, when suddenly someone taps you on the shoulder.

t_ch: 哟，贵客！怎么样，在宴会上喝得可算尽兴？
t_en: Hey, honored guest! So, did you drink to your heart's content at the party?

t_ch: 要是闲着，不如来和我们…嗯，你手里拿着的是什么？
t_en: If you're free, why don't you join us... hmm, what's that you're holding?

t_ch: 八卦是人们不变的天性。你将维吉妮娅托你给阿波罗尼送信一事如实相告……
t_en: Gossip is a constant in human nature. You told the truth about how Verginia asked you to deliver a message to Apollonius...

t_ch: 哈哈！小维吉妮娅一向藏不住心思，只是未曾将其挑明。
t_en: Haha! Little Verginia has always worn her heart on her sleeve, even if she never said it out loud.

t_ch: 这回她终于要正面出击了吗…真叫人兴奋哪！
t_en: Is she finally going to make her move... This is so exciting!

t_ch: 他们是怎么认识的？
t_en: How did they meet?

t_ch: 以前，维吉妮娅曾作为外交使者前往树庭，目睹阿波罗尼独辩四贤人。
t_en: Once, Verginia visited the Grove as a diplomatic messenger and witnessed Apollonius debating against four Sages.

t_ch: 那回，他少有地发怒了：「既然你们较之世界命运更在乎瑟希斯的存亡，我便先毁了它的神体！」
t_en: That time, in a rare display of anger, he declared: "Since you care more about Cerces' survival than the world's destiny, I shall destroy their divine form first!"

t_ch: 他抄起投石索，将沉石向瑟希斯的神体掷去，吓得其余贤人抱头鼠窜。
t_en: He grabbed a sling and lobbed a heavy stone at Cerces's divine form, sending the other Sages scurrying away in panic.

t_ch: 小维吉妮娅也被吓到了，很久没敢和他搭话。但在相处以后，才知道阿波罗尼是个格外温厚的家伙。
t_en: Little Verginia was also scared and didn't dare talk to him for a long time. But after getting to know him, she discovered that Apollonius was actually an exceptionally gentle fellow.

t_ch: 她写的每一首诗，阿波罗尼都会细细装裱，并给予她鼓励。
t_en: Apollonius would carefully frame every poem she wrote and give her encouragement.

t_ch: 不过那家伙，待谁都是这样温和热情，真是不开窍啊。
t_en: But he is so warm and friendly to everyone, he's completely clueless.

t_ch: 有我帮忙，顽石也得开窍。
t_en: With my help, even a rock would get a clue.

t_ch: 嗑学家们，放心交给我吧。
t_en: Shippers, leave it to me.

t_ch: 其实，我们并非看不见小维吉妮娅心中柔软之处，只是无从触及。
t_en: To be honest, we can see the tenderness in little Verginia's heart... we just can't reach it.

t_ch: 上个月，小维吉妮娅用悬锋古语给拉比努斯写了首赞美诗，他竟然念反了！
t_en: Last month, little Verginia wrote a Praisesong in Ancient Kremnoan for Labienus, and he read it backwards!

t_ch: 你这天空蛮夷笑什么？她精心给你挑选的头巾，你居然绑在杖上当战旗！
t_en: What are you laughing at, you sky barbarian? She carefully picked out that headscarf for you, and you went and tied it to your staff like a battle flag!

t_ch: …总之，贵客，真的拜托你了。
t_en: ...Anyway, esteemed guest, I'm really counting on you.

t_ch: 我们…不，我…该怎么办好呢……
t_en: What should we... no, what should I... do...

t_ch: 啊，{NICKNAME}阁下，抱歉，我想事情的时候会有些入神。
t_en: Ah, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}, my apologies. I tend to get lost in thought sometimes.

t_ch: 曳石爵，有一封给你的信。
t_en: Dux Helkolithist, there's a letter for you.

t_ch: 看这信纸的折法，是小维吉妮娅吧，一定又写了漂亮的新诗……
t_en: Looking at how this letter is folded, it must be from little Verginia. She must have written another beautiful poem...

t_ch: 阿波罗尼展开信笺，细细阅读了一番。
t_en: Apollonius unfolds the letter and reads it carefully.

t_ch: 阁下，辛苦你送信了。
t_en: Thank you for delivering the letter.

t_ch: 你看了信，都没情感波动吗？
t_en: Did you read the letter and feel nothing at all?

t_ch: 你这呆瓜，是有什么心事吗？
t_en: You silly goose, is something on your mind?

t_ch: 阁下如果不嫌弃的话，我为你讲些小维吉妮娅的往事吧。
t_en: If you don't mind, I'd like to tell you some stories about little Verginia.

t_ch: 她是个爱打扮爱写诗的姑娘，经常和金织爵讨论服饰，也经常和命运爵共写歌谣。
t_en: She's a girl who loves to dress up and write poetry, often discussing fashion with Dux Goldweaver and frequently composing ballads with Dux Fatorum.

t_ch: 只是，她的天性从小得不到包容。她害怕打仗，害怕震耳欲聋的吼声，害怕金属交击的脆响……
t_en: However, her nature was never accepted since childhood. She feared battles, feared the deafening roars, feared the crisp clang of clashing metal...

t_ch: 那些宏伟的诗篇，在悬锋城只是擦拭锋刃的废纸。
t_en: Those grand poems are just scrap paper for wiping blades in Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 遭部族驱逐后，小维吉妮娅加入了逐火，这才找到自己的归身之处。
t_en: After being banished from her tribe, little Verginia joined the Flame-Chase Journey before she finally found a place to belong.

t_ch: 是个好孩子。
t_en: She's a good kid.

t_ch: 那么，你呢？
t_en: So, what about you?

t_ch: 她是如风一般的诗人，能去到任何想去的地方，也能在任何地方逗留。至于我……
t_en: She's a poet like the wind, free to go wherever she wants and linger wherever she pleases. As for me...

t_ch: …阁下，你先回去吧，就说我还在考虑。拜托你了。
t_en: ...You should head back. Just tell her I'm still thinking about it. I'm counting on you.

t_ch: 你将阿波罗尼的回应告诉维吉妮娅，她看上去有些失落，但在你的安慰下很快振作了。
t_en: You tell Verginia about Apollonius's response. She looks a little let down, but quickly cheers up after your consolation.

t_ch: 而到了下一个门扉时……
t_en: When the next Entry Hour arrives...

t_ch: 阿波罗尼失踪了？什么时候？
t_en: Apollonius is missing? Since when?

t_ch: 是的…昨天{NICKNAME}阁下送完信回来，我才决定鼓起勇气和他当面表达清楚。
t_en: Yes... After {NICKNAME} came back from delivering the letter yesterday, I finally decided to gather my courage and tell him face-to-face.

t_ch: 但是，哪里都找不到阿波罗尼的身影，用传信石版也、也联系不上他……
t_en: But Apollonius was nowhere to be found, and I couldn't even reach him through the teleslate...

t_ch: 那家伙，该不会是潜逃了吧？
t_en: That idiot, did he just run away?

t_ch: 当务之急，是分头把他找到。{NICKNAME}贵客，实在不好意思，得拜托你也搭把手了。
t_en: The most urgent matter now is to split up and find him. {NICKNAME}, I'm terribly sorry, but I'll have to ask for your help with this too.

t_ch: 城下的古遗迹里有一台雅努斯迷津，对阿波罗尼最后的目击记录就在那附近。
t_en: There's a Janus' Maze in the ancient ruins below the city, and the last sighting of Apollonius was recorded around there.

t_ch: 也许凭借贵客岁月祭司的力量，能够找到阿波罗尼的踪迹吧。
t_en: Perhaps with your power as a Priest of Time, we can track down Apollonius.

t_ch: 记得友爱之馆录有这雅努斯迷津的使用方法，让我回忆回忆……
t_en: I remember the Library of Philia has records about how to use Janus' Maze. Allow me a moment to recall...

t_ch: {RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}万径之门{RUBY_E#}啊，请指引我——「于梦中惊觉的悬石，该去往何方？」
t_en: {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Gate of Infinity{RUBY_E#}, please guide me — "Where should the floating stone that awakens from dreams go?"

t_ch: 门径的导引是——「欢宴之出口，虚妄的边境」……
t_en: The guidance from the Gate is — "The egress of banquets, the border of futility"...

t_ch: 果然…雅努斯并未指引我，只是坚定了我心中的选择…那便无需再犹豫了。
t_en: As expected... Janus didn't guide me, but rather reinforced the choice I had already made in my heart... No need to hesitate any longer.

t_ch: 「欢宴之出口，虚妄的边境」…他是想，离开斯缇科西亚？！
t_en: "The egress of banquets, the border of futility"... Is he planning to leave Styxia!?

t_ch: 身为幻境的一部分，脱离幻境一定会消失的…必须赶紧阻止他！
t_en: As a part of the illusion, he'll vanish if he leaves... I must stop him quickly!

t_ch: 看起来这台雅努斯迷津还能够继续使用，不过只能等有机会再回来探索了。
t_en: Looks like this Janus' Maze is still operational, but we'll have to come back and explore it another time.

t_ch: 当初打算离开树庭的时候，似乎也是这样的心情呢。只是，这次的出走，我竟然犹豫了这么久啊。
t_en: I had the same feeling back when I first planned to leave the Grove. But this time... I can't believe I hesitated for so long about running away.

t_ch: 离开这里，你会死的！
t_en: You'll die if you leave this place!

t_ch: 享受这场无休的宴会，不好吗？
t_en: Why not enjoy this never-ending party?

t_ch: 啊，{NICKNAME}阁下…真不愧是你，能在这里找到我。
t_en: Ah, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}... I knew you would be the one to find me here.

t_ch: 但你的反应，再度证明了雅努斯迷津为我揭露的真相——
t_en: But your reaction reaffirms the truth that Janus' Maze revealed to me—

t_ch: 这场欢宴是一场幻境，而我是幻境的一簇泡沫。一旦离开此处，我就会消失得无影无踪。
t_en: This banquet is but an illusion, and I am merely a bubble in this fantasy. Once I leave this place, I will vanish without a trace.

t_ch: 既然如此，为什么你要……
t_en: If you knew that, why did you...

t_ch: 因为我是一位贤人，我是凯撒的参谋，我是逐火的黄金裔。
t_en: It's because I am a Sage, the Imperator's Advisor, and a Flame-Chasing Chrysos Heir.

t_ch: 在畅饮蜜酿之时，我脑中掠过一缕针扎般的念头——逐火之旅果真能如此完美么？一场欢宴，果真能承载翁法罗斯那悲惨的命运么？
t_en: As I indulged in the honey brew, a needle-sharp thought pierced my mind — could the Flame-Chase Journey really be this perfect? Could a mere banquet truly bear the weight of Amphoreus's tragic destiny?

t_ch: 贤人一旦产生了这样的忧虑，则无法安享欢宴，并必然踏上追求真相而自毁的道路。
t_en: Once a Sage harbors such worries, they can no longer enjoy the banquet, and will inevitably embark on a self-destructive path in pursuit of truth.

t_ch: 想想朋友们，想想维吉妮娅……
t_en: Think about our friends, think about Verginia...

t_ch: …抱歉，阁下，正因为我想着他们，才必须离开这里。
t_en: ...I'm sorry, but it's precisely because I care about them that I must leave this place.

t_ch: 此前我同你说过，维吉妮娅是风一般的诗人。而我…我是山崖上的一枚顽石。
t_en: As I told you before, Verginia is a poet like the wind. And I... I am but a stubborn boulder on a cliff.

t_ch: 顽石对微风的轻拂无动于衷，只因它心系世界的命运……
t_en: The stubborn boulder remains unmoved by the gentle breeze, for its heart is bound to the world's destiny...

t_ch: 如今，风已有归处。不只是她，还有塞涅卡、拉比努斯…一路走来，他们都太辛苦了。
t_en: Now, the wind has found her place. Not just her, but Seneca, Labienus too... they've all been through so much on this journey.

t_ch: 用这场宴会作为他们的终点，一定是<color=#dbc291ff>那位伙伴</color>的仁慈。
t_en: Making this banquet their final destination must be <color=#dbc291ff>that companion's</color> act of mercy.

t_ch: 欢宴的主人，海瑟音……
t_en: Hysilens, the master of the feast...

t_ch: 原来，你已经知道这么多了。
t_en: So, you already know this much.

t_ch: 是的。自产生疑虑至今，我已经知道得太多太多。我是宴会中的杂音，我眉间的忧愁会让我爱的人们也失去笑容，甚至将他们也拽出这方美景……
t_en: Yes. Since doubts first arose, I've learned far too much. I'm a discordant noise at a party — the worry between my brows steals smiles from those I love, even dragging them away from this beautiful scene...

t_ch: 这便是理性的顽石必须滚落山崖的原因——它不忍打扰另一侧，那微风吹拂的静谧湖面。
t_en: That's why the stubborn boulder of reason must tumble down the cliff — it can't bear to disturb the serene lake surface on the other side, where gentle breezes blow.

t_ch: ……我明白了。
t_en: ...I understand.

t_ch: 再见，曳石爵。
t_en: Farewell, Dux Helkolithist.

t_ch: 可你消失了，他们该怎么办……
t_en: But what about them if you disappear...

t_ch: 关于这点，或许是最不用担心的。
t_en: That's probably the least of our worries.

t_ch: 这本是一场完美的、无休的宴会，无人会哭泣、无人应哀愁，唯有蜜酿沁人心脾、唯有欢歌引人酣醉。
t_en: It was meant to be a perfect, endless banquet, where no one would cry or grieve, where only honey brew would warm the heart, and only joyous songs would entice the soul.

t_ch: 正如那传说中的再创世。你说对吗…海瑟音？
t_en: Just like the legendary Era Nova. Isn't that right... Hysilens?

t_ch: 阿波罗尼，你这家伙跑哪里去了？让我们一通好找。
t_en: Apollonius, where have you been? We've been looking all over for you!

t_ch: 抱歉，我脑子还有些涨，兴许是蜜酿喝得昏过去，不知道在哪儿睡着了吧。让各位操心了。
t_en: Sorry, my head's still a bit woozy. Must've passed out from too much honey brew and dozed off somewhere. Thanks for worrying about me.

t_ch: 听说也劳{NICKNAME}阁下你费了些劲，请容我郑重向你致歉。
t_en: I heard you went through quite some trouble, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}. Please allow me to offer you my sincere apologies.

t_ch: 你…真的是阿波罗尼？
t_en: Are you... really Apollonius?

t_ch: 我当然是阿波罗尼，树庭贤人、逐火军参谋，这确凿无疑。
t_en: I am, without a doubt, Apollonius, Sage of the Grove and advisor to the Flame-Chasers.

t_ch: 你…没事吧？
t_en: You... are you okay?

t_ch: 嗯，当然没事，我还想接着与大家欢享这场盛宴呢。
t_en: Hmm, of course I'm fine. I'd love to keep enjoying this banquet with everyone.

t_ch: 那，维吉妮娅的告白……
t_en: So, about Verginia's confession...

t_ch: 告白…？
t_en: A confession...?

t_ch: 阿波罗尼，也记得向小维吉妮娅道个歉。她还以为你是不知道怎么面对她才闹失踪，自责了好一会儿呢。
t_en: Apollonius, don't forget to apologize to little Verginia too. She thought you went missing because you didn't know how to face her and has been blaming herself for quite a while.

t_ch: 小维吉妮娅，你……
t_en: Little Verginia, you...

t_ch: 我、我并没有自责，阿波罗尼也无需道歉。只是，我想在大家的见证下，再、再、再试一次……
t_en: I—I'm not blaming myself, and Apollonius doesn't need to apologize. It's just that, I want to t—t—try again... with everyone here as witness...

t_ch: 阿波罗尼，请允许我为你朗诵一首赞诗吧。这是我…在心底深埋了许久的诗。
t_en: Apollonius, please allow me to recite a poem of praise for you. This is a poem that I've... kept buried deep in my heart for so long.

t_ch: 维吉妮娅闭上眼，定神半晌，再度睁眼时气场已截然不同。
t_en: Verginia closes her eyes and remains focused for a while. When she opens her eyes again, her demeanor has completely changed.

t_ch: 「树影衬着智者的背影，像一颗巨石在背负年轮。」
t_en: "The shadow of a tree frames the sage's back, like a boulder bearing the rings of time."

t_ch: 「鸟儿的歌是穿林的风声，却绕不过他嶙峋的裂痕。」
t_en: "The bird's song is a whisper of wind through the forest, but it cannot pass the fractures in his form."

t_ch: 「他的重量，压住了鸟儿的翅膀…和萌动的心。」
t_en: "His weight presses down on the bird's wings... and the stirring heart within."

t_ch: 小维吉妮娅，在过去的很长一段时间里，我都以含而不露的理性自居。
t_en: Little Verginia, for a long time, I've prided myself on my subtle and restrained rationality.

t_ch: 但我一直想说，你念诗的样子很美。我想过，等到了新世界，一定要与你并肩相依……
t_en: But I've always wanted to tell you, you look beautiful when you recite poetry. I've dreamed that when we reach the new world, we'll stand side by side...

t_ch: 为什么…为什么总要等呢？这里有你、有我、有大家…这里分明就是新世界啊！
t_en: Why... why do we always have to wait? We're all here — you, me, everyone... This is clearly our new world!

t_ch: …对不起，阿波罗尼，我有些失态了……
t_en: ...I'm sorry, Apollonius, I got a bit carried away...

t_ch: 不，小维吉妮娅，你说得对，这里就是新世界。我的意思是…也许是时候，直面并希求你那澎湃的心灵了。
t_en: No, little Verginia, you're right, this is the new world. What I mean is... maybe it's time to face and embrace that passionate soul of yours.

t_ch: 恭喜你呀，小维吉妮娅，让这块石头开窍可真不容易！
t_en: Congratulations, little Verginia! It wasn't easy getting through that thick skull!

t_ch: 应该是恭喜你们两位…唉，真叫人欣慰啊，*天空粗口*！
t_en: I should congratulate you two... *sigh* How heartwarming, *Skyfolk swear word*!

t_ch: …恭喜你！
t_en: ...Congrats!

t_ch: 谢谢大家，谢谢你们，谢谢……
t_en: Thank you everyone, thank you all, thank you...

t_ch: 恭喜你们，我的伙伴……
t_en: Congratulations, my friends...

t_ch: 你好，灰鱼儿。所谓欢宴，正是由灵魂的洋流交织而出的浪花与暗涌。
t_en: Hello, Gray Fry. A feast, you see, is like waves and undercurrents woven from the ocean currents of our souls.

t_ch: 你我如今所目睹的，便是一朵格外洁白而美丽的浪花…不是吗？
t_en: What we're witnessing right now is an exceptionally pure and beautiful whitecap... don't you think?

t_ch: 这是你自导自演的幻境……
t_en: This is just a fantasy of your own making...

t_ch: 曳石爵…是你复原的，对吗？
t_en: Dux Helkolithist... you restored him, didn't you?

t_ch: 我并未刻意去做些什么。在永恒酣歌的斯缇科西亚，法吉娜的神力会满足人们所有的愿望。
t_en: I didn't deliberately do anything. In Styxia, where eternal songs resound, Phagousa's divine power fulfills all wishes.

t_ch: 阿波罗尼心系世界命运，故总会产生疑虑，最终必然离开欢宴，消为一簇泡沫。
t_en: Apollonius, burdened by the world's destiny, is forever plagued by doubts, destined to leave the banquet and dissolve into a bubble of foam.

t_ch: 而维吉妮娅只愿将浪漫吐露与心上人，哪怕知晓彼此只是记忆催生的蜃景，必不动摇半分。
t_en: And Verginia would only share her romance with her beloved. Even knowing they were only an illusion born of memories, her resolve would not waver in the slightest.
